'''Elesa''' was a small mining town on [[Ando Prime/Legends|Ando Prime]].

==History==
With little more than ten buildings, two roads and a handful of living spaces, the tiny settlement nevertheless served as the capital city of the planet.
Elesa was located some 500 kilometers from the [[Juaka Canyon]], a natural gorge under the control of native [[Talid/Legends|Talids]].<ref name="sj">{{GamerCite|2|Snow Job}}</ref>

Elesa was mostly inhabited by grumpy [[Human/Legends|Human]] miners who favored heavy clothing and beards. Notable local business at some point before [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]] included two cantinas, the [[Cliffside]] and the [[Icicle]]; a repair shop tended by [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] female [[Feena Ghel]]; and the [[Miner's Union Hall]], a bureaucratic office managed by [[Geon Justic]]. The law was enforced by local constable [[Byam Awson]].<ref name="sj" />

Around that time, aspiring crimelord [[Kaeline Ungasan]] organized a podracing event on Ando Prime, the [[Ungasan Cross Country]], both to wrestle the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] control over the planet and to cover his illegal mining operation of [[Vonium/Legends|Vonium]]. Ungasan sent his minion, [[Ree]], to Elesa to bribe the Miner's Union representative — although Ree claimed to meet with Justic because of Ree's job with a zoological research team.<ref name="sj" />

In 27 BBY, Ungasan's criminal activities on Ando Prime were discovered and exposed, in the first of several podracing scandals. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] reporters traveled to Elesa to cover the growing banning of podracing through the galaxy.<ref name="h">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|sports/13328_1.html|Podracing Faces Uncertain Future}}</ref>
